column1: # wootc E2E test matrix — Windows editions × bootc images.
# Columns (tab-separated): tier  name  image  win_version  win_edition  win_key
#   tier         smoke | full   (run-matrix.sh --tier selects; smoke ⊆ full)
#   win_key      Generic product key that picks the edition from the ISO.
#                phase3=on     — pass --phase3 (rung-3 graduate onto blank disk)
# The bitlocker axis (WOOTC_E2E_BITLOCKER=on) drops PreventDeviceEncryption so
# Windows auto-encrypts C:; wootc must then place root.disk on a separate
# unencrypted volume rather than forcing a decrypt (SPEC 3.5).
# The image axis covers every base family + bootloader (grub2 vs systemd-boot),
# incl. a composefs image (projectbluefin/dakota); run-e2e derives the bootloader
# and desktop; the Windows axis covers 10/11 across Pro/Home/Enterprise/LTSC.
# Keep smoke small (one proven baseline + the two riskiest axes); full is the
# ── backend axis — the three deployment flavors × Windows versions ──
# composefs-native systemd-boot (dakota). Each must also prove Phase 3.
# ── Windows axis (baseline EL10 GNOME image) — editions & versions ──
# Home + Device Encryption: the nervous-user default on new consumer machines —
# BitLocker's Home-edition flavor, auto-enabled, no recovery-key literacy.
